title: Number Formatter
description: Format numbers in US, EU, Japanese unit (万/億/兆), and currency formats all at once. Free online number formatting tool.

howToUse.label: Enter a number
howToUse.description: Type the number you want to format. Commas and spaces in the input are automatically stripped.

howToUse.label: Choose decimal places
howToUse.description: Select 0–4 decimal places. Currency formats (JPY/USD/EUR) use their standard decimal settings.

howToUse.label: Copy the format you need
howToUse.description: Seven formats are displayed at once. Click the Copy button next to the format you want.

faq.question: How do I add comma separators to a number?
faq.answer: Enter your number and the 'US (comma separated)' row shows it as 1,234,567. Click Copy to grab it instantly.

faq.question: What are the Japanese unit formats (万/億/兆)?
faq.answer: Japanese text commonly expresses large numbers in units of 万 (10,000), 億 (100,000,000), and 兆 (1,000,000,000,000). For example 123456789 becomes 1億2345.7万.

faq.question: What is the EU number format?
faq.answer: In many European countries, periods (.) are used as thousands separators and commas (,) as decimal separators. For example: 1.234.567,89.

faq.question: Can I format numbers as currency?
faq.answer: Yes. Japanese Yen (¥), US Dollar ($), and Euro (€) are supported with the correct symbol placement and standard decimal handling via the Intl.NumberFormat API.

faq.question: Is my data sent to a server?
faq.answer: No. All formatting is done in your browser using the built-in Intl.NumberFormat API. No data is sent anywhere.
